title: Flaky Dutch Chicken Flatbread
description: A citrusy and crumbly Dutch dish featuring thyme, perfect for a solo dinner, made with long fermentation dough and high protein friendly ingredients, served in a bento box.
instructions: Combine flour, yeast, and salt in a large mixing bowl to make the dough.
Gradually add water to the dough and mix until a shaggy mass forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Let the dough ferment for 12 hours in a warm place.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the dough into a large rectangle and place on a baking sheet.
Top the dough with chicken breast, thyme, lemon zest, garlic, and feta cheese.
Drizzle olive oil over the top of the flatbread.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown.
Let the flatbread cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving in a bento box.
